title: Meet the middle clue
narration: Binary search starts by checking the middle of sorted data instead of checking every spot one by one.
callout: The middle number is the first big clue.

title: Too small means go right
narration: If the middle is 7 and the target is 9, the left half is too small. We keep only the right half.
decision: 7 is too small, so move left to mid + 1.

predictionPrompt.prompt: If the middle value is too big, what should happen next?

choices.label: Keep the left half
choices.feedback: Correct. A too-big middle means the target must be smaller.

choices.label: Keep the right half
choices.feedback: Not this side. If the middle is too big, the answer cannot be to the right.

title: Sorted order makes this possible
narration: We can throw away half only because the values stay in order. Without sorting, the middle would not give a safe clue.
callout: Sorted order is the rule that makes the middle useful.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why is sorted order the most important rule for binary search?
